raw_consumer_statement:
I am a verified cardholder who became a victim of identity theft affecting both my American Express Platinum and Gold accounts. I submitted a police report and certified documentation, only to have over {$3900.00} withdrawn from my checking account during an active fraud investigationwhile I was enrolled in Amexs XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX. I was assured no payments would be due, yet my accounts were closed without resolution. I later received a letter stating a dispute had been processed in error, with no further explanation. 
What followed was a breakdown of trust and security. Ive received relentless scam callsmany from individuals mimicking Amex representatives with XXXX XXXXXXXX XXXXasking for verification of my PIN and Social Security number. Some appeared to know internal Amex information. Ive also received spoofed emails, letters referencing unfamiliar accounts, and phone calls that began with Hello instead of Amexs official greeting. Despite all of this, Amex never requested multi-factor authentication, never froze the account properly, and never followed up on my fraud claim. 
My credit has been destroyed. My personal information appears to have been cloned or compromised, and I suspect XXXX spoofing or counterfeit card usage. I have spoken to dozens of representativesmostly XXXXmany of whom gave conflicting information, and none of whom followed through. This is not a minor error. Its systemic negligence.
